title: What's Not Included in Your Commercial Cleaning Quote (And What to Ask Before You Sign)
excerpt: Most commercial cleaning quotes look reasonable until the extras start appearing on your invoice. Here's what's typically left out of a standard quote, and the questions you should ask before committing to a cleaner in Newcastle.
content:
You get a commercial cleaning quote. The price looks reasonable. You sign.

Then a few months in, you get charged extra for carpet cleaning. The windows still haven't been touched. The kitchen deep clean you assumed was part of the deal — turns out it's an add-on.

This happens all the time with commercial cleaning contracts in Newcastle. Not because cleaners are deliberately misleading you, but because what's "standard" varies a lot depending on who you're talking to. Here's what to watch for before you put pen to paper.

## Routine Cleaning vs Periodic Cleaning — They're Not the Same Thing

Most commercial cleaning quotes cover routine cleaning — the regular scheduled visits, usually daily or a few times a week. This typically includes:

- Vacuuming and mopping floors
- Wiping down desks and surfaces
- Emptying bins
- Cleaning toilets and sinks
- Basic kitchen wipe-down

What it usually doesn't include is periodic cleaning — the deeper, less frequent jobs. Things like:

- Carpet steam cleaning
- Window cleaning (inside and out)
- Blind cleaning
- Upholstery cleaning
- Kitchen degreasing
- High dusting above head height

These are almost always quoted separately, and they're the ones that catch businesses off guard when they haven't asked upfront. If your lease requires you to maintain the premises in good condition — and most commercial leases in NSW do — these periodic jobs are not optional.

## Consumables — Who's Paying?

Another common grey area: consumables.

That means bin liners, toilet paper, hand soap, paper towels. Some cleaners include them in the quote. Most don't. Some supply them but add a markup on top. Some expect you to provide everything and they just do the cleaning.

This matters for your budget. A medium-sized office on Hunter Street can burn through a surprising amount of consumables in a month. Work it out before you sign, not after your first invoice lands.

## After-Hours, Weekends, and Public Holidays

Most commercial cleaning in Newcastle happens after business hours — that's standard. But if you need cleaning done at a specific time, or if you need a clean over a long weekend or public holiday, check whether that attracts a higher rate.

Some contracts roll it into the standard price. Others charge penalty rates on top. If your business operates outside the usual Monday to Friday setup — a medical centre in Charlestown, a gym in Kotara, a hospitality venue in the CBD — this is worth nailing down before you commit.

## What Happens When Something Gets Missed

This is the question nobody thinks to ask, and it's one of the most important.

If your cleaner skips a task — or just does it badly — what's the process? Do they come back? Do you get a credit on your next invoice? Is there any real accountability built into the contract?

A decent cleaning company will have a clear process for this. A supervisor doing regular spot checks. A way for you to log issues and get them sorted quickly. If a cleaner can't tell you how they handle complaints before you sign, that tells you something.

## The Insurance Question

Commercial cleaning means people working in your space, often unsupervised, after hours. Make sure they're properly insured.

Ask for proof of public liability insurance — $10 million cover is standard in Australia. Check their workers' compensation is current too. If someone gets injured in your office during a clean and the contractor isn't properly covered, it can become your problem in a hurry.

This isn't about distrust. It's basic due diligence.

## Staff Turnover and Site Knowledge

High staff turnover is common across the cleaning industry. When it happens, the quality of your clean can change overnight — a new person who doesn't know your site, doesn't know your preferences, and hasn't been briefed properly.

Ask how the company handles this. Do they brief new staff before putting them on your site? Is there a site checklist? Does a supervisor visit regularly to check the standard?

A detailed site checklist and regular supervisor drop-ins keep the standard consistent even when the team changes. It's the difference between a clean that stays good and one that slowly slides.

## Questions to Ask Before You Sign

To save yourself the headache later:

- What's included in the routine quote, and what's charged extra?
- Are consumables included, or do I supply them?
- What are the rates for public holidays and weekends?
- How do I report an issue, and what's your process for fixing it?
- Can I see your current public liability and workers' compensation certificates?
- How do you manage staff changes on my site?

Get the answers in writing — ideally in the contract itself, not just a verbal assurance.

## Don't Just Go With the Cheapest Quote

The cheapest quote is almost always cheap for a reason. Commercial cleaning is something you'll notice every single day — in how your space looks, how it smells, how your team and clients feel when they walk in.

A cleaner cutting their price is usually cutting corners somewhere. Whether that's rushing through jobs, using cheaper products, or skimping on supervision — it shows up eventually.

If you're looking for commercial cleaning in Newcastle and want a straight conversation about what's actually included — no surprises, no hidden extras — talk to the YOS cleaning team.
